heading: Statement of origin of disease or injury: limitations
label: 1219 Statement of origin of disease or injury: limitations
text: A member of an armed force may not be required to sign a statement relating to the origin, incurrence, or aggravation of a disease or injury that he has. Any such statement against his interests, signed by a member, is invalid.
notes: 1962ActSections 1218 and 1219 are restated, without substantive change, to conform to the style adopted for title 10.

Amendments1962— substituted “Statement of origin of disease or injury: limitation” for “Statement against interest void” in section catchline, and “A member of an armed force may not be required to sign a statement relating to the origin, incurrence, or aggravation of a disease or injury that he has. Any such statement against his interests, signed by a member, is invalid” for “No person in the Armed Forces may be required to sign a statement of any nature relating to the origin, incurrence, or aggravation of any disease or injury he may have. Any such statement against his own interest, whenever signed, is of no force and effect.”Pub. L. 87–651
Effective DateSection effective , see , .Jan. 1, 1958section 2301 of Pub. L. 85–5671 Stat. 172
